...
Yifan Zhou

Mohamed El Mistiri, PhD
...
Sachin Grover and Heni Ben Amor from Arizona State University for all

great discussions, meetings, and lots and lots of experiments that led to this

. #LLM #RL #Neurips2025 #ProcterAndGamble NeurIPS 2025
...
Learners 🤯!
...
Surprisingly, we find in our paper that LLMs can solve RL tasks without the need for any external component!
...
We introduce Prompted Policy Search (ProPS), an RL method based only LLMs and in-context learning. What’s cool about ProPS is that you can provide it with prior knowledge about the task domain or hints about how to solve the task in natural language. Imagine giving a robot a book to start from, rather than learning from scratch. The result is a family of reinforcement learning algorithms that combines linguistic and numerical optimization. ProPS shows SOTA performance and is also able to explain in language each policy update!

results.title: Mohammad Reza Yousefi
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist @ P&G -- Computer Vision · Image Analysis · VLMs · Agentic Systems · Foundation Models
...
Senior Data Scientist at [Procter & Gamble](https://www.linkedin.com/company/procter-and-gamble)
...
Data scientist and AI/ML engineer with 15+ years across applied research and production software systems. My strongest area is modern computer vision, particularly the augmentation of vision-language models with specialised perception components to push them beyond what they can do alone. Broader work spans foundation models, agentic systems, multimodal AI, and cloud-native production deployment. I currently lead end-to-end AI projects across R&D and IT in Procter & Gamble corporate functions, partnering with multiple business units. The focus is on the parts of the work that matter most: architecture and design choices, performance and cost optimisation in cloud environments, evaluation methodology, and the cross-functional partnership with scientists, ML engineers and domain experts that gets a system from prototype to product. My background spans industry, startups and academic research, with deep history across computer vision, sequence learning with LSTMs - the era directly preceding today's Transformer-based LLMs - and hardware-accelerated deep learning.
...
### Senior Data Scientist at [Procter & Gamble](https://www.linkedin.com/company/procter-and-gamble) (Current)

- Replatformed a legacy computer-vision pipeline onto a foundation-model architecture; devised cost-control and token-minimisation methods for production LLM workloads.
- Fine-tuned a segmentation foundation model into a domain-specialised, prompt-free segmenter for an internal imaging workflow; distributed multi-GPU training, managed cloud-endpoint deployment.

CASFER is nurturing the leading scientists of the future, like Deepa Bhuvanagiri , who serves as the Student Leadership Council Treasurer. She is a Computer Science PhD student at Case Western Reserve University. She is conducting research in the Solar Durability Lifetime Extension lab (SDLE) under the guidance of Dr. Roger French . Deepa’s current research is centered on applying data science and analytics methodologies to problems in the geospatial domain. Her past work includes building temperature maps of the shallow subsurface of the continental United States for degradation assessments. Deepa's passion is in the intersection between human centered problems and data science as evident in her research which ranges from topics in human behavior to environmental problems.  

She is a founding member of the CASFER Student Leadership Council, and currently she serves as the SLC Treasurer. We can’t wait to see all that Deepa accomplishes during her time with CASFER. #casfer #nsf #nsfstories #slc

Our focus is on identifying cutting-edge computational chemistry, process systems models, and machine learning techniques that create value for P&G. Our recent key achievements include: Successfully integrated advanced machine learning, data science, and AI models in an application that accurately predicts product temperature profiles during transportation, leading to critical business decisions that resulted in multimillion-dollar savings. Implemented Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and Large Language Models (LLM) to automate the product registration process, streamlining document preparation to meet the requirements of various country Boards of Health and potentially saving millions. Developed virtual formulation applications that combine historical experimental data with AI models to predict stable new formulations, achieving significant cost savings over the years Show less

As a Scientist at Procter & Gamble, I work on developing novel products and processes in the consumer goods industry, leveraging AI for data analysis in consumer behaviour, identifying consumer trends and market product validation. I have contributed to several innovation projects prior to my time at P&G, such as a nano-composite medical material design and biological flow study on one cell organisms. I have also gained experience in technical market analysis, identifying consumer needs and translating them into product characteristics. I hold a Master of Science in Chemical Engineering from the University of Belgrade, where I graduated summa cum laude and conducted biomedical research. I also completed a Code in Place program in Computer Programming from Stanford University, where I learned the fundamentals of Python development. I've also pursued courses and done work in web3, blockchain, and have written smart contracts. I am constantly learning and exploring new technologies and trends, and I aim to guide and manage innovation projects that can create positive impact and value.

results.title: Procter & Gamble CIO Just Explained How AI and Data Will Drive the Company’s Next Growth Cycle | TIKR.com
highlights:
What that framing misses went on display at the Evercore Consumer & Retail Conference on June 10. Seth Cohen, P&G’s Chief Information Officer, spent nearly an hour explaining the architecture of what may be the most sophisticated consumer intelligence and AI deployment in packaged goods. The conversation covered a proprietary 35-petabyte consumer behavioral database, digital twin modeling that has cut R&D timelines from five-plus years to under six months, AI-driven media buying outperforming agency work at lower cost, and a supply chain automation initiative running unattended manufacturing operations.
...
at Evercore was not about brands
...
SAP (enterprise software that manages financial, supply chain, and operational data) running as a single global instance is unusual at P&G’s scale. Most multinationals run dozens of regional environments that do not share data cleanly. P&G’s unified setup means every market and category feeds into the same data layer, which powers what Cohen calls the company’s AI factory: the central platform where AI models are built and deployed across all business units.
...
The underlying data is substantial. P&G logs over 2 million consumer touch points per year, including thousands of connected homes with IoT sensors (internet-connected devices that track real-world product usage). Cohen described a database spanning approximately 35 petabytes, built partly from wrist sensors measuring how consumers wash their hair and motion patterns feeding behavioral models. From that data, P&G has built digital twins of consumers, not statistical averages but individual simulations, that allow product concepts to be tested before physical prototypes are built.
...
build extends well beyond R&D. Cohen described a fundamental restructuring of how P&G creates and buys advertising.
...
Where P&G once refreshed TV advertising one to four times a year, the shift to digital and social now requires 10 to 200 times that volume of content per category. Outsourcing at that scale to agencies is not economically viable, so P&G has internalized the creative pipeline. Generative AI now takes a brief to a finished asset, adapts it automatically to every platform’s technical requirements, and feeds it into media buying tools that have also been brought in-house.
...
Cohen also detailed GEO, or generative engine optimization, which means ensuring P&G’s brands appear accurately in AI-powered search tools like ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and Amazon’s Alexa. Unlike traditional SEO (search engine optimization, which targets keyword matching), GEO requires that the data feeding the AI answers is accurate, complete, and aligned with how consumers phrase questions in natural language.
...
’s categories
...
P&G’s Supply Chain 3.0 initiative is running what the company calls unattended operations: portions of manufacturing shifts where AI and automation allow plants to run without workers on the floor. The entire shift cannot be automated, but specific portions of each production day now operate unattended. IoT sensors on production lines flag quality issues before they become defects, adjusting line parameters in real time.
...
Cohen also confirmed that P&G is already using AI to run financial forecasting without human input in select use cases, with plans to expand across R&D, selling, and product supply.
...
The TIKR
...
model prices P&G at around $203 by June 30, 2030, built on a revenue CAGR of around 3.6% annually. The two primary growth drivers are Fabric & Home Care (the largest segment by revenue) and the Beauty segment, which reported 7% organic sales growth in Q3 FY2026 per P&G’s April 24 earnings release. The margin driver is operating leverage from the AI and productivity investments Cohen described at Evercore, with the mid-case assuming net income margins near 19.5%.
...
